summary: Recommend replacing the current hardcoded Codex init shortlist in `src/control_tower/config_ui.py` with exactly these five options, in order: `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.2`. This is the best current blend of official OpenAI guidance and current local Codex CLI reality: official docs say to start with `gpt-5.4` and use `gpt-5.4-mini`/`gpt-5.4-nano` for cheaper lower-latency work, while the local Codex CLI v0.117.0 visible model list currently prioritizes `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4-mini`, then legacy-but-still-listed Codex-optimized fallbacks (`gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.2`).
work_completed: Inspected the current init code and verified that the codex backend shortlist is hardcoded in `src/control_tower/config_ui.py` under `BACKEND_MODELS["codex"]`, while project defaults in `src/control_tower/templates/project/state/project.json` do not currently pin a default model.
Confirmed the current repository still exposes stale Codex options: `o4-mini`, `o3`, `codex-mini-latest`, `gpt-4.1`, and `o4-mini-high`.

Verified current official OpenAI model guidance from primary docs: the Models page says to start with `gpt-5.4` for complex reasoning/coding and use smaller `gpt-5.4-mini` or `gpt-5.4-nano` variants for lower-latency, lower-cost workloads.
Verified current official OpenAI model catalog still lists Codex-family legacy models including `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex`, and marks `codex-mini-latest` as deprecated.

Checked current local Codex CLI behavior via `codex --help` and the local Codex model cache fetched on 2026-03-31, which shows the visible shortlist ordered by priority as `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`.

Identified one UX implementation caveat in the init prompt: `_prompt_backend_and_model()` labels the first option as default, but when `default_model` is blank, pressing Enter currently returns an empty model override rather than the first listed model.

findings.message: Current code location: `src/control_tower/config_ui.py:15-22` hardcodes the Codex shortlist, and `src/control_tower/config_ui.py:424-456` renders the backend-specific model picker. `src/control_tower/templates/project/state/project.json:34-39` carries Codex execution defaults but no model list.

Primary-source basis: OpenAI's Models docs currently say, 'If you're not sure where to start, use gpt-5.4' and present `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4 mini`, and `gpt-5.4 nano` as the current frontier family for coding and lower-cost variants. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models and https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/all .

Primary-source basis for legacy Codex names: the official model catalog still lists `GPT-5.3-Codex`, `GPT-5.2-Codex`, `GPT-5.1 Codex`, `GPT-5.1-Codex-Max`, `GPT-5.1 Codex mini`, and marks `codex-mini-latest` as deprecated. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/all .

Current local Codex CLI evidence favors the same top two picks and then older Codex-specific fallbacks. In the local Codex model cache fetched at `2026-03-31T05:54:42Z`, the visible priority order is `gpt-5.4` (1), `gpt-5.4-mini` (3), `gpt-5.3-codex` (5), `gpt-5.2-codex` (8), `gpt-5.2` (9), `gpt-5.1-codex-max` (10), `gpt-5.1-codex-mini` (19).

Do not use `gpt-5.4-nano` in the default top-five yet. It is current and official in API docs, but it is not exposed in the current local Codex CLI visible shortlist. It is better treated as a future optional/custom entry unless Tower is intentionally optimizing for cheap subagents over general coding quality.
Do not keep `codex-mini-latest` in the curated defaults. The official model catalog labels it deprecated, and the local Codex CLI no longer exposes it as a visible current choice.

Naming caveat: UI copy should distinguish display labels from stored slugs. The docs use human labels like 'GPT-5.4 mini' and 'GPT-5 mini', while Codex config should store exact model ids such as `gpt-5.4-mini`. Using slugs in config and friendly labels in the picker will reduce confusion.

Legacy alias caveat: `gpt-5-codex` and `gpt-5-codex-mini` still exist in local Codex metadata but are hidden and carry upgrade metadata pointing users toward `gpt-5.4`. They should not be shown as init defaults.

Implementation caveat: the picker currently prints the first item as '(default)' but returns an empty override when Enter is pressed and `default_model` is blank. If Tower wants the first curated option to be the real init default, the prompt logic needs to seed or return that first slug explicitly.

follow_up_recommendations: Keep the existing `custom` escape hatch so advanced users can still type `gpt-5.4-nano`, `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, or future ids manually.
Render friendly labels but persist exact slugs, for example `GPT-5.4 (recommended)` -> `gpt-5.4` and `GPT-5.4 Mini` -> `gpt-5.4-mini`.
Remove stale options `o4-mini`, `o3`, `gpt-4.1`, `o4-mini-high`, and deprecated `codex-mini-latest` from the curated Codex picker.
When implementing, fix `_prompt_backend_and_model()` so the displayed default and the returned default are the same behavior.
memory_worthy: As of 2026-03-31, the best current Tower init shortlist for the Codex backend is `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.2`.

Official OpenAI docs now position `gpt-5.4` as the default starting point and `gpt-5.4-mini`/`gpt-5.4-nano` as the lower-cost frontier variants, while local Codex CLI still visibly lists older Codex-specific fallback models.
`codex-mini-latest` is deprecated and should not appear in Tower init defaults.
